    17. WORLD BUILDING (PART III): YOUR WORLD VS. YOUR TOOLS
</h2>

  <!-- Attachment Blocks -->
<!-- DEBUG: attachments.count=2 -->

  <!-- DEBUG: attachment.id=42453227, kind=text -->
  <div class="lecture-attachment lecture-attachment-type-text" id="lecture-attachment-42453227">
    <div class="attachment-data"></div>
    
      <div class="attachment-data"></div>
<div class="lecture-text-container">
<p></p><p>The first thing you have to decide is where is HOMEBASE for your WORLD.</p><p>For some of you it will be the "real world".</p><p>For example... A brick and mortar business.</p><p>Or a studio.</p><p>Or an event.</p><p>Or a product.</p><p>For some of you it's home will be online.</p><p>Like a website you build.</p><p>Or an app.</p><p>For some of you, your WORLD will live on someone else's platform.</p><p>Maybe your WORLD will live on Instagram. (side-note, never forget that Instagram's WORLD is an app that lives on someone else's platform. The IPHONE. No Steve Jobs, no Instagram. So when people complain about #freethenipple it makes me laugh because they forget that Instagram has to play by Apple's non-nudity rules. Their whole business model is built on someone else's platform.)</p><p>Or your WORLD may live on YouTube.</p><p>But the first thing is understanding where your home experience lives (YOUR WORLD). Because everything that is NOT your WORLD will be a TOOL to take your audience back home.</p><p>Don't confuse ATTRACTION TOOLS with YOUR WORLD.</p><p>Wherever people go to experience your art AND PAY YOU FOR IT is your WORLD.</p><p>Everything else should be treated like an ATTRACTION TOOL.</p><p>Understanding the distinction will help you manage your priorities every day.</p><p>If you find yourself investing more time on Instagram (for example) than in your WORLD, then you have your priorities wrong.</p><p>Or if your TOOLS aren't directly leading people to discover your WORLD (but instead you're using platform only to "post fun things"), then you're not treating it like a business.</p><p>(That doesn't mean every post has to promote. No, no. But build a strategy. For example... Build a story of posts that leads up to an invitation into your world. Ever notice that everything Drake posts leads up to an executable action on his fans part? That's how YOU need to behave.) </p><p>But understand that what's a WORLD for some, may be a TOOL for others. And vice versa.</p><p>Take for example YouTube:</p><h2>Most people use YouTube to promote things. But YouTubers use the platform as their WORLD. (That's what generates income for them.) So they use platforms like Instagram etc... to bring their audience back to YOUTUBE where all the action happens. But eventually a YouTuber may get a talk-show. Like Lilly Singh (former YouTuber with her own NBC television show now) And at that point they may decide to use what was once their WORLD (YouTube) as an ATTRACTION TOOL now. <em>They've successfully expanded their brand energy.</em>
</h2><p>Get it?</p><h1>1. Know the homebase of the WORLD you're building. (Where you make (or will make) your money.)</h1><h1>2. Figure out what ATTRACTION TOOLS you should use to attract people to your WORLD.</h1><p>How do you choose with so many options available out there?</p><p>By knowing who your audience is. (No, it isn't 'everybody'.)</p><p>By figuring out where your audience hangs out.</p><p>If they hang out at coffee shops, you need to hang out there too and find a way to bring them into your WORLD.</p><p>If they hang out on Instagram, you need to develop a presence on IG that serves to bring them back into your WORLD.</p><p>Figure out where your potential fans hang out... And hang out there too.</p><p>But never forget... That's not HOME.</p><p>You're only using this hangout "place" as a place to meet them. </p><p><a href="https://learn.lastnightsparty.tv/courses/enroll/lectures/23573896" rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank">But how do you identify your true fans among all the masses of people going by?</a></p><p></p>
